new_classes.label_en: other still wine of fresh grapes in containers > 2 L (CN 2204 22 93)
new_classes.label_de: anderer Stillwein aus frischen Weintrauben in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2204 22 93)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes without a protected designation of origin (PDO) or protected geographical indication (PGI), and not Tokaj wine, presented in containers with a nominal volume greater than 2 litres, classified under CN 2204 22 93. The total dry matter content (non-volatile substances) is determined in accordance with Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22, encompassing substances such as sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring agents, and salts.

A quality borne by a wine of fresh grapes representing the sum of non-volatile substances (including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring agents, and salts) as defined in Additional Note 4 A to Chapter 22 of the EU Combined Nomenclature.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the key distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 22 93: (1) it is a wine of fresh grapes (parent class), (2) presented in containers greater than 2 litres (LargeContainer part restriction), (3) without PDO or PGI (complement restrictions on quality), and (4) the total dry matter quality per Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22 is represented by TotalDryMatterQuality. However, the actual quantitative thresholds for dry matter content (as specified in Additional Note 4A, which distinguishes sub-codes within the 22042222–22042298 range by g/L of residual sugar/dry extract) could not be captured without introducing numeric data range restrictions on a dry-matter data property, which is not yet in the TBox. Additionally, the explicit exclusion of Tokaj wine (CN 2204 22 33) from this sub-range is not formally asserted. These gaps prevent a coverage score of 1.0.
